title: Quorum Queues and Replicated Delivery Claims · Language Lens: Majority Loss Recovery
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must compute majority availability, leader disruption, poison limits, confirm evidence, and restore limits without overstating safety; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the majority loss recovery increment in a Go quorum-availability and delivery-limit model. L…
objectives: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Measure queue leader placement, client connection locality, cross-node routing, rebalance, and single-core hot-path constraints; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Majority Loss Recovery · Theory Majority Loss Recovery
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 5785 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to compute majority availability while separating quorum custody from stakeholder effect; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then reuse a message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Plan majority loss, delayed confirms, redelivery, operator checks, replica repair, and residual data-risk evidence without unsafe force recovery; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
